title: Mitigating allocative tradeoffs and harms in an environmental justice data tool

meta_data.Data_Name: Paper-level data deposit from Harvard Dataverse DOI 10.7910/DVN/EVWNC2
meta_data.Data_summary: An archived replication package containing the datasets used in the study workflow to reproduce and audit CalEnviroScreen and to run the funding impact and sensitivity analyses.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: All data used in this work are publicly available online and all datasets used are archived at https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/EVWNC2 ref. 54. Location: Data availability Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_summary: Census tract-level environmental exposure and population vulnerability indicators used to compute CalEnviroScreen composite scores and disadvantaged-community designations, including pollution measures and health and socioeconomic variables used for sensitivity analyses and model reproduction.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: we used census tract-level data obtained from the current version of CalEnviroScreen version 4.0 implemented in late 2021 consisting of 8,035 observations with 21 variables. Location: Methods Data Confidence: high
meta_data.Geographic_Coverage: United States > California > Census tracts statewide

meta_data.Data_summary: Census tract-level CalEnviroScreen composite scores and percentile rankings from the earlier CalEnviroScreen version used as the forcing variable and treatment assignment for the regression discontinuity funding impact analysis.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: we examined years 2017–2021 of the California Climate Investments funding dataset and used CalEnviroScreen 3.0 scores implemented in 2017. Location: Methods Data Confidence: high
meta_data.Geographic_Coverage: United States > California > Census tracts statewide

meta_data.Data_summary: Small-area modeled health survey estimates used to add tract-level prevalence variables for multiple conditions and to substitute survey-based respiratory and cardiovascular indicators into alternative CalEnviroScreen model specifications.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: we used the PLACES dataset from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention to include tract-level variables on estimated prevalence for asthma cancer chronic kidney disease and coronary heart disease. Location: Methods Data Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_Name: American Community Survey ACS tract-level demographic data
meta_data.Data_summary: Survey-based socioeconomic and demographic estimates at the census tract level used to measure race and ethnicity composition and poverty and as covariates in causal analyses and subgroup audits.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: We obtained demographic information on tract-level race ethnicity from the American Community Survey. Location: Methods Data Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_Name: California Climate Investments CCI funding dataset
meta_data.Data_summary: Administrative records of California Climate Investments funded projects and awarded amounts used to compute total funding allocated per census tract and to estimate the causal effect of CalEnviroScreen disadvantaged-community designation on funding.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: we examined years 2017–2021 of the California Climate Investments funding dataset and used CalEnviroScreen 3.0 scores implemented in 2017. Location: Methods Data Confidence: high
meta_data.Geographic_Coverage: United States > California > Census tracts statewide
